missing_sub_functions.description: Validates that all mandatory fields (first name, last name, username, phone number, email, password, confirm password) are not empty and display inline error messages.
Verifies that registration with all valid inputs succeeds, returns a success response, and triggers sending of verification email.
Confirms that a verification email is sent upon successful registration and contains a valid token.

unclear_categories.reason: Single category covering multiple distinct error types (invalid email, weak password, mismatched passwords, invalid phone/username, duplicate email) makes test intent too broad; consider splitting per error type for clarity.
Phone number format rule is undefined; tests rely on assumed patterns which may change. Priority and test data need revision once specification is clarified.
Username format rule is undefined; tests rely on assumed patterns which may change. Priority and test data need revision once specification is clarified.
Resend verification email policy (e.g., rate limits, cooldown) is not defined; test intent assumes functionality may not be implemented as expected.

recommendations: Add a sub-function 'Required Field Validation' with test categories for empty/missing mandatory fields to ensure coverage of all fields.
Add a positive test category 'Successful Registration' under registration to validate end-to-end happy path.
Add a test category 'Verification Email Sent' to confirm that the system dispatches the verification email after registration.
Consider splitting CAT004 into separate categories per validation error type to improve test traceability and clarity.
Update CAT007 and CAT008 once the exact format rules for phone number and username are defined; mark as low priority until then.
Clarify open questions regarding email verification process (expiration, resend policy, retry limits) and adjust test categories accordingly.
Include test categories for HTTP status codes as per assumptions (e.g., 400 for validation errors, 409 for duplicate email) once API contract is defined.
